1.1: In the beginning God created heaven and earth.
1.2: And the earth was void and vacant, and darkness was upon the face of the depth: and the Spirit of God moved over the waters.
1.3: And God said: Be light made. And light was made.
1.4: And God saw the light that it was good: and he divided the light from the darkness.
1.5: And he called the light, Day, and the darkness, Night: and there was evening & morning, that made one day.
1.6: God also said: Be a firmament made amidst the waters: and let it divide between waters and waters.
1.7: And God made a firmament, and divided the waters that were under the firmament, from those that were above the firmament. And it was so done.
1.8: And God called the firmament, Heaven: and there was evening & morning that made the second day.
1.9: God also said: let the waters that are under heaven be gathered together into one place: and let the dry land appear. And it was so done.
1.10: And God called the dry land, Earth, and the gathering of waters together, he called Seas. And God saw that it was good.
1.11: And said: Let the earth shoot forth green herbs, and such as may seed, & fruit-trees yielding fruit after his kind, such as may have seed in itself upon the earth. And it was so done.
1.12: And the earth brought forth green herb, such as seedeth according to his kind, and tree that beareth fruit, having seed each one according to his kind. And God saw that it was good.
1.13: And there was evening and morning that made the third day.
1.14: Again God said: Be there lights made in the firmament of heaven, to divide the day and the night, and let them be for signs & seasons, and days and years;
1.15: to shine in the firmament of heaven, and to give light upon the earth. And it was so done.
1.16: And God made two great lights; a greater light to govern the day, and a lesser light to govern the night; and stars.
1.17: And he set them in the firmament of heaven, to shine upon the earth,
1.18: and to govern the day & the night, and to divide the light and the darkness. And God saw that it was good.
1.19: And there was evening and morning that made the fourth day.
1.20: God also said: Let the waters bring forth creeping creature having life, and flying fowl, over the earth under the firmament of heaven.
1.21: And God created huge whales, and all living and moving creature, that the waters brought forth according to each sort, and all fowl according to their kind. And God saw that it was good.
1.22: And he blessed them saying: Increase and multiply, and replenish the waters of the sea: and let the birds be multiplied upon the earth.
1.23: And there was evening and morning that made the fifth day.
1.24: God said moreover: Let the earth bring forth living creature in his kind, cattle, and such as creep, and beasts of the earth according to their kinds: and it was so done.
1.25: And God made the beasts of the earth according to their kinds, and cattle, and all that creepeth on the earth in his kind. And God saw that it was good,
1.26: and he said Let us make Man to our image & likeness: and let him have dominion over the fishes of the sea, and the fowls of the air, and the beasts, and the whole earth, and all creeping creature that moveth upon the earth.
1.27: And God created man to his own image: to the image of God he created him, male and female he created them.
1.28: And God blessed them, and saith: Increase and multiply, and replenish the earth, and subdue it, and rule over the fishes and the sea, and fowls of the air & all living creatures that move upon the earth.
1.29: And God said: Behold I have given you all manner of herb that seedeth upon the earth, and all trees that have in themselves seed of their own kind, to be your meat:
1.30: and to all beasts of the earth, and to every fowl of the air, and to all that move upon the earth, and wherein there is life, that they may have to feed upon. And it was so done.
1.31: And God saw all things that he had made, and they were very good. And there was evening and morning that made the sixth day.

2.1: The heavens therefore & the earth were fully finished, and all the furniture of them.
2.2: And the seventh day God ended his work which he had made; and rested the seventh day, from all work that he had done.
2.3: And he blessed the seventh day & sanctified it: because in it he had ceased from all his work which God created to make.
2.4: These are the generations of heaven & earth, when they were created in the day, when our Lord God made the heaven, and the earth,
2.5: and every plant of the field, before it shot up in the earth, and every herb of the ground before it sprang. For our Lord God had not rained upon the earth; and man was not to till the earth:
2.6: But a spring rose out of the earth, watering all the overmost part of the earth.
2.7: Our Lord God therefore formed man of the slime of the earth: and breathed into his face the breath of life, & man became a living soul.
2.8: And our Lord God had planted Paradise of pleasure from the beginning: wherein he placed man whom he had formed.
2.9: And our Lord God brought forth of the ground all manner of trees, fair to behold, and pleasant to eat of: the tree of life also in the middle of Paradise: and the tree of knowledge of good and evil.
2.10: And a river issued out of the place of pleasure to water Paradise, which from thence is divided into four heads.
2.11: The name of one is Phison: that is it which compasseth all the land of Hevilath, where gold groweth.
2.12: And the gold of that land is very good: there is found bdellium, and the stone onyx.
2.13: And the name of the second river is Gehon: that is it which compasseth all the land of Ethiopia.
2.14: And the name of the third river is Tigris: that same passeth along by the Assyrians. And the fourth river, the same is Euphrates.
2.15: Our Lord God therefore took man, & put him in the Paradise of pleasure, to work, & keep it.
2.16: And he commanded him saying: Of every tree of Paradise eat thou:
2.17: But of the tree of knowledge of good & evil eat thou not. For in what day soever thou shalt eat of it, thou shalt die the death.
2.18: Our Lord God also said: It is not good for man to be alone: let us make him a help like unto himself.
2.19: Our Lord God therefore having formed of clay all beasts of the earth, and fowls of the air; brought them to Adam, that he might see what to call them: for all that Adam called any living creature, the same is his name.
2.20: And Adam called all beasts by their names, and all fowls of the air, and all cattle of the field: but unto Adam there was not found an helper like himself.
2.21: Our Lord God therefore cast a deep sleep upon Adam: and when he was fast asleep, he took one of his ribs, & filled up flesh for it.
2.22: And our Lord God built the rib which he took of Adam into a woman, & brought her to Adam.
2.23: And Adam said: This now is bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh: she shall be called woman, because she was taken out of man.
2.24: Wherefore man shall leave his father & mother, & shall cleave to his wife, & they shall be two in one flesh.
2.25: And they were both naked, to wit Adam and his wife; and were not ashamed.

3.1: But the serpent also was more subtile than all the beasts of the earth, which our Lord God had made. Which said to the woman: Why hath God commanded you, that you should not eat of every tree of Paradise?
3.2: To whom the woman answered: Of the fruit of the trees that are in Paradise we do eat:
3.3: but of the fruit of the tree which is in the midst of Paradise, God hath commanded us that we should not eat: and that we should not touch it, lest perhaps we die.
3.4: And the serpent said to the woman: No, you shall not die the death.
3.5: For God doth know that in what day soever you shall eat thereof, your eyes shall be opened: and you shall be as gods, knowing good and evil.
3.6: The woman therefore saw that the tree was good to eat, and fair to the eyes, and delectable to behold: and she took of the fruit thereof, & did eat, & gave to her husband, who did eat.
3.7: And the eyes of them both were opened: and when they perceived themselves to be naked, they sowed together leaves of a fig tree, and made themselves aprons.
3.8: And hearing the voice of our Lord God walking in Paradise at the afternoon air, Adam hid himself, and so did his wife from the face of our Lord God, amidst the trees of Paradise.
3.9: And our Lord God called Adam, and said to him: Where art thou?
3.10: Who said: I heard thy voice in Paradise: and I feared, because I was naked, and I hid me.
3.11: To whom he said: And who hath told thee that thou wast naked, but that thou hast eaten of the tree, whereof I commanded thee that thou shouldst not eat?
3.12: And Adam said: The woman which thou gavest me to be my fellow-companion, gave me of the tree, & I did eat.
3.13: And our Lord God said to the woman: Why hast thou done this? Who answered: The serpent deceived me, and I did eat.
3.14: And our Lord God said to the serpent: Because thou hast done this thing, accursed art thou among all cattle, and beasts of the earth: upon thy breast shalt thou go, & earth shalt thou eat all the days of thy life.
3.15: I will put enmity between thee & the woman, & thy seed and the seed of her: she shall bruise thy head in pieces, and thou shalt lie in wait of her heel.
3.16: To the woman also he said: I will multiply thy travails and thy child-bearings: in travail shalt thou bring forth children, and thou shalt be under thy husband's power, and he shall have dominion over thee.
3.17: And to Adam he said: Because thou hast heard the voice of thy wife, and hast eaten of the tree wherefore I commanded thee that thou shouldst not eat, cursed is the earth in thy work: with much toiling shalt thou eat thereof all the days of thy life.
3.18: Thorns and thistles shall it bring forth to thee, and thou shalt eat the herbs of the earth.
3.19: In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread, till thou return to earth, of which thou wast taken: because dust thou art, and into dust thou shalt return.
3.20: And Adam called the name of his wife, Eve: for because she was mother of all the living.
3.21: Our Lord God also made for Adam and his wife garments of skins, and clothed them:
3.22: And said: Lo Adam is become as it were one of us, knowing good & evil: now therefore lest perhaps he reach forth his hand, and take also of the tree of life, and eat, and live for ever.
3.23: And our Lord God sent him out of the Paradise of pleasure, to work the earth of which he was taken.
3.24: And he cast out Adam, and placed before the Paradise of pleasure Cherubins, and a flaming and a turning sword, for to keep the way of the tree of life.

4.1: And Adam knew Eve his wife, who conceived and brought forth Cain, saying: I have gotten a man through God.
4.2: And again she brought forth his brother Abel. And Abel was a shepherd, & Cain a husbandman.
4.3: And it befell after many days that Cain offered of the fruits of the earth gifts to our Lord.
4.4: Abel also offered of the first-begotten of his flock, and of their fat: and our Lord had respect of Abel, & to his gifts.
4.5: But to Cain, and to his gifts he had not respect: And Cain was exceeding angry, and his countenance abated.
4.6: And our Lord said to him: Why art thou angry? and why is thy countenance fallen?
4.7: If thou do well, shall thou not receive again: but if thou doest ill, shall not thy sin forthwith be present at the door? But the lust thereof shall be under thee, and thou shalt have dominion over it.
4.8: And Cain said to Abel his brother: Let us go forth abroad. And when they were in the field, Cain rose up against his brother Abel, and slew him.
4.9: And our Lord said to Cain: Where is Abel thy brother? Who answered: I know not, am I my brother's keeper?
4.10: And he said to him: what hast thou done? The voice of thy brother's blood cryeth to me out of the earth.
4.11: Now therefore cursed shalt thou be upon the earth, which hath opened her mouth, & received the blood of thy brother at thy hand.
4.12: When thou shalt till it, it shall not yield to thee her fruit: a rogue and vagabond shalt thou be upon the earth.
4.13: And Cain said to our Lord: Mine iniquity is greater than that I may deserve pardon.
4.14: Lo thou dost cast me out this day from the face of the earth, and from thy face shall I be hid, and I shall be a vagabond and fugitive on the earth: every one therefore that findeth me shall kill me.
4.15: And our Lord said to him: No, it shall not so be: but whosoever shall kill Cain, shall be punished seven-fold. And our Lord put a mark on Cain, that whosoever found him should not kill him.
4.16: And Cain went forth from the face of our Lord, and dwelt as a fugitive on the earth at the east side of Eden.
4.17: And Cain knew his wife, who conceived and brought forth Enoch: And he built a city, and called the name thereof by the name of his son, Enoch.
4.18: Moreover Enoch begat Irad, and Irad begat Maviael, and Maviael begat Mathusael, and Mathusael begat Lamech.
4.19: Who took two wives, the name of the one was Ada, and the name of the other Sella.
4.20: And Ada brought forth Iabel, who was the father of them that dwell in tents, and of herdsmen.
4.21: And his brother's name was Jubal: he was the father of them that sing on harp & organs.
4.22: Sella also brought forth Tubalcain, who was a hammerer and worker in all work of brass and iron. And the sister of Tubalcain was Noema.
4.23: And Lamech said to his wives Ada and Sella: Hear my voice ye wives of Lamech, harken to my talk: for I have slain a man to the wounding of myself, and a stripling to mine own dry blow bruising.
4.24: Seven-fold vengeance shall be taken of Cain: but of Lamech seventy times seven-fold.
4.25: Adam also knew his wife again: and she brought forth a son, & called his name Seth, saying: God hath given me other seed for Abel, whom Cain slew.
4.26: But to Seth also was born a son, whom he called Enos, this man began to invocate the name of our Lord.

5.1: This is the book of the generation of Adam. In the day, when God created man, to the likeness of God made he him.
5.2: Male and female created he them; and blessed them: and called their name Adam, in the day when they were created.
5.3: And Adam lived an hundred and thirty years; & begat to his own image and likeness, and called his name Seth.
5.4: And the days of Adam, after he begat Seth, came to eight hundred years: and he begat sons and daughters.
5.5: And all the time that Adam lived, came to nine hundred & thirty years, and he died.
5.6: Set also lived an hundred five years, and begat Enos.
5.7: And Seth lived after he begat Enos, eight hundred and seven years, and begat sons and daughters.
5.8: And all the days of Seth came to nine hundred & twelve years, and he died.
5.9: And Enos lived ninety years, and begat Cainan.
5.10: After whose birth he lived eight hundred and fifteen years, and begat sons and daughters.
5.11: And all the days of Enos came to nine hundred and five years, and he died.
5.12: Cainan also lived seventy years, & begat Malaleel.
5.13: And Cainan lived after he begat Malaleel, eight hundred & forty years, and begat sons and daughters.
5.14: And all the days of Cainan came to nine hundred and ten years, and he died.
5.15: And Malaleel lived sixty five years and begat Jared.
5.16: And Malaleel lived after he begat Jared, eight hundred & thirty years, and begat sons and daughters.
5.17: And all the days of Malaleel came to eight hundred ninety five years, and he died.
5.18: And Jared lived an hundred sixty two years, and begat Enoch.
5.19: And Jared lived after he begat Enoch eight hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.
5.20: And all the days of Jared came to nine hundred sixty two years, and he died.
5.21: Moreover Enoch lived sixty five years, and begat Mathusala.
5.22: And Enoch walked with God: and lived after he begat Mathusala, three hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.
5.23: And all the days of Enoch came to three hundred sixty five years.
5.24: And he walked with God, and was seen no more: because God took him.
5.25: Mathusala also lived an hundred eighty seven years, and begat Lamech.
5.26: And Mathusala lived, after he begat Lamech, seven hundred eighty two years, and begat sons and daughters.
5.27: And all the days of Mathusala came to nine hundred sixty nine years, and he died.
5.28: And Lamech lived an hundred eighty two years, & begat a son:
5.29: and he called his name Noe, saying: This son shall comfort us from the works & labours of our hands on the earth, which our Lord cursed.
5.30: And Lamech lived, after he begat Noe, five hundred ninety five years, and begat sons and daughters.
5.31: And all the days of Lamech came to seven hundred seventy seven years, and he died. And Noe when he was five hundred years old, begat Sem, Cham, and Japhet.

6.1: And after that men began to be multiplied upon the earth, and had procreation of daughters,
6.2: the sons of God seeing the daughters of men that they were fair, took to themselves wives out of all which they had chosen.
6.3: And God said: My spirit shall not remain in man for ever, because he is flesh: and his days shall be an hundred and twenty years.
6.4: And Giants were upon the earth in those days. For after the sons of God did company with the daughters of men, & they brought forth children, these be the mighty of the old world, famous men.
6.5: And God seeing the malice of men was much on the earth, & that all the cogitation of their heart was bent to evil at all times,
6.6: it repented him that he had made man on the earth. And touched inwardly with sorrow of heart,
6.7: I will, saith he, clean take away man, whom I have created, from the face of the earth, from man even to beasts, from that which creepeth even unto the fowls of the air. For it repenteth me that I have made them.
6.8: But Noe found grace before our Lord.
6.9: These are the generations of Noe: Noe was a just and perfect man in his generations, he did walk with God.
6.10: And he begat three sons, Sem, Cham, and Japheth.
6.11: And the earth was corrupted before God, & was replenished with iniquity.
6.12: And when God had perceived that the earth was corrupted (for all flesh had corrupted his way upon the earth)
6.13: He said to Noe: The end of all flesh is come before me, the earth is replenished with iniquity from the face of them, and I will destroy them with the earth.
6.14: Make thee an ark of timber plank: cabinets shalt thou make in the ark, and shalt pitch it within and without with bitume.
6.15: And thus shalt thou make it. The length of the Ark shall be three hundred cubits: fifty cubits the breadth, and thirty cubits the height of it.
6.16: Thou shalt make a window in the ark, & in a cubit finish the top of it: & the door of the ark thou shalt set at the side below, middle chambers & third lofts shalt thou make in it.
6.17: Behold I will bring the waters of a great flood upon the earth, that I may destroy all flesh wherein there is breath of life under heaven. All things that are in the earth shall be consumed.
6.18: And I will establish my covenant with thee: and thou shalt enter into the ark, thou and thy sons, and thy wife, and the wives of thy sons with thee.
6.19: And of all living creatures of all flesh, thou shalt bring pairs into the ark, that they may live with thee: of the male sex and the female.
6.20: Of fowls according to their kind, and beasts in their kind, and of all that creepeth on the earth, according to their kind: pairs of all sorts shall enter in with thee, that they may live.
6.21: Thou shalt take therefore with thee of all meats, that may be eaten, and thou shalt lay them up with thee: & they shall be meat for thee and them.
6.22: Noe therefore did all things which God commanded him.

7.1: And our Lord said to him: Get thee in, thou and all thy house into the ark; for I have seen thee just in my sight in this generation.
7.2: Of all beasts that are clean, thou shalt take seven and seven, male and female:
7.3: but of the beasts that are unclean two and two, male and female. Yea and of the fowls also of the air seven and seven, male and female: that seed may be saved upon the face of the whole earth.
7.4: For yet a while, and after seven days I will rain upon the earth forty days and forty nights: and I will clean destroy all substance that I have made, from the face of the earth.
7.5: Noe therefore did all things which our Lord had commanded him.
7.6: And he was six hundred years old, when the waters of the flood flowed over the earth.
7.7: And Noe entered and his sons, his wife and the wives of his sons with him into the ark, because of the waters of the flood.
7.8: Of beasts also the clean and the unclean, and of fowls, and of all that moveth upon the earth.
7.9: two and two went to Noe into the ark, male and female, as our Lord had commanded Noe.
7.10: And after the seven days were passed, the waters of the flood flowed over the earth.
7.11: In the six hundred year of the life of Noe, in the second month, in the seventeenth day of the month, all the fountains of the great depth were broken up, and the flood-gates of heaven were opened:
7.12: and the rain fell upon the earth forty days and forty nights.
7.13: In the very point of that day entered Noe, and Sem, and Cham, and Japheth his sons; and his wife, and the three wives of his sons with them into the ark.
7.14: they and every beast according to their kind, and all cattle in their kind, and all that moveth upon the earth according to their kind, and all fowl according to their kind, all birds, and all that fly.
7.15: went to Noe into the ark, two and two of all flesh, wherein there was breath of life.
7.16: And such as entered in, male and female of all flesh did enter in, as God had commanded him: and our Lord shut him in on the out side.
7.17: And the flood grew forty days upon the earth: and the waters increased, and lifted up the ark on high from the earth.
7.18: For they overflowed exceedingly; and filled all on the face of the earth: moreover the ark fleeted upon the waters.
7.19: And the waters prevailed out of measure upon the earth; and all the high mountains under the whole heaven were covered.
7.20: Fifteen cubits higher was the water above the mountains, which it covered.
7.21: And all flesh was consumed that moved upon the earth, of fowl, of cattle, of beasts, and of all creepers, that creep upon the earth: all men,
7.22: and all things, wherein there is breath of life on the earth, died.
7.23: And he clean destroyed all substance that was upon the earth, from man even to beast, as well it that creepeth, as the fowls of the air: and they were destroyed from off the earth: but only Noe remained, and they that were with him in the ark.
7.24: And the waters held on above the earth an hundred fifty days.

8.1: And God remembered Noe, and all the beasts, and all the cattle which were with him in the ark, and brought a wind upon the earth, and the waters decreased.
8.2: And the fountains of the depth and the flood-gates of heaven were shut up: and the rain from heaven was stayed.
8.3: And the waters returned from the earth going & coming: and they began to decrease after an hundred fifty days.
8.4: And the ark rested the seventh month, the seven and twentieth day of the month, upon the mountains of Armenia.
8.5: But the waters for all that were going and decreasing until the tenth month: for in the tenth month, the first day of the month, the tops of the mountains appeared.
8.6: And after that forty days were passed, Noe opening the window of the ark, which he had made, let forth a crow:
8.7: which went forth, and did not return, till the waters were dried upon the earth.
8.8: He sent forth also a dove after him, to see if the waters were ceased yet upon the face of the earth.
8.9: Which finding not where her foot might rest, returned to him into the ark: for the waters were upon the whole earth: and he stretched forth his hand and caught her, and brought her into the ark.
8.10: And having expected yet seven more days again he let forth a dove out of the ark.
8.11: But she came to him at eventide, carrying a bough of an olive tree that had green leaves, in her mouth. Noe therefore understood that the waters were ceased upon the earth.
8.12: And he expected yet nevertheless other seven days: and he sent forth a dove, which returned not any more unto him.
8.13: Therefore in the sixth hundred and one year, the first month, the first day of the month, the waters were clean diminished upon the earth: & Noe opening the roof of the ark, looked, and saw that the face of the earth was dried.
8.14: In the second month, the seven and twentieth day of the month the earth was dried.
8.15: And God spake to Noe, saying:
8.16: Go forth of the ark, thou and thy wife, thy sons and the wives of thy sons with thee.
8.17: All cattle that are with thee, of all flesh, as well in fowls, as in beasts, and all creepers that creep upon the earth, bring out with thee, and go ye upon the earth: increase and multiply upon it.
8.18: Noe therefore went forth, and his sons: his wife, and the wives of his sons with him.
8.19: Yea and all cattle, beasts, & creepers that creep upon the earth, according to their kind, went forth out of the ark.
8.20: And Noe built an Altar to our Lord: and taking of all cattle and fowls that were clean, offered Holocausts upon the Altar.
8.21: And our Lord smelled a sweet savour, and said: I will no more curse the earth for men: for the sense and cogitation of man's heart are prone to evil from their youth: I will no more therefore strike every living soul as I have done.
8.22: All the days of the earth, seed-time and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, night and day shall not rest.

9.1: And God blessed Noe and his sons, And he said to them: Increase & multiply, and replenish the earth.
9.2: And your terror and dread be it upon all the beasts of the earth, and upon all the fowls of the air, with all that move upon the earth: all the fishes of the sea are delivered to your hand.
9.3: And all that moveth and liveth shall be yours for meat: even as the green herbs have I delivered all to you.
9.4: Saving that flesh with blood you shall not eat.
9.5: For I will require the blood of your souls at the hands of all beasts: and at the hand of man, at the hand of each man, and of his brother, will I require the soul of man.
9.6: Whosoever shall shed man's blood, his blood shall be shed: for to the image of God man was made.
9.7: But increase you and multiply, and go upon the earth, and fill it.
9.8: Thus also said God to Noe, and his sons with him:
9.9: Behold I will establish my covenant with you, and with your seed after you:
9.10: and with every living soul that is with you, as well in all fowls as in cattle & beasts of the earth that are come forth out of the ark, and in all beasts of the earth.
9.11: I will establish my covenant with you, and all flesh shall be no more destroyed with the waters of a flood, neither shall there be from hence-forth a flood to waste the earth.
9.12: And God said: This is the sign of the covenant which I give between me and you, and between every living soul, that is with you, for perpetual generations:
9.13: my bow will I set in the clouds, and it shall be the sign of a covenant between me and between the earth.
9.14: And when I shall cover the element with clouds, my bow shall appear in the clouds:
9.15: and I shall remember my covenant with you, and with every living soul that beareth flesh: and there shall no more be waters of a flood, to destroy all flesh.
9.16: And my bow shall be in the clouds, and I shall see it, and I shall remember the everlasting covenant, that was made between God and every living soul of all flesh which is upon the earth.
9.17: And God said to Noe: This shall be the sign of the covenant, which I established between me & all flesh of the earth.
9.18: The sons therefore of Noe, that came out of the ark, were Sem, Cham, and Japheth: and Cham he is the father of Chanaan.
9.19: These three are the sons of Noe: and of these was all mankind spread over the whole earth.
9.20: And Noe a husbandman began to till the ground, and planted a vineyard.
9.21: And drinking of the wine was made drunk, and naked in his tabernacle.
9.22: Which when Cham the father of Chanaan had seen, to wit that his father's privities were bare, he told it to his two brethren abroad.
9.23: But indeed Sem and Japhet put a cloak upon their shoulders, and going backward, covered the privities of their father: and their faces were turned away, and they saw not their father's privities.
9.24: And Noe awaking from the wine, when he had learned what his younger son had done to him,
9.25: he said: Cursed be Chanaan, a servant of servants shall he be unto his brethren.
9.26: And he saith: Blessed be the Lord God of Sem, Chanaan be his servant.
9.27: God enlarge Japheth, and dwell he in the tabernacles of Sem, and Chanaan be his servant.
9.28: And Noe lived after the flood three hundred fifty years.
9.29: And all his days were in the whole nine hundred fifty years: and he died.

10.1: These are the generations of the sons of Noe, Sem, Cham, and Japheth: and children were born to them after the flood.
10.2: The children of Japheth: Gomer, & Magog, & Madai, & Javan, & Tubal, and Mosoch, and Thiras.
10.3: Moreover the children of Gomer: Ascenez, and Riphath, and Thogorma.
10.4: And the sons of Javan: Elisa, and Tharsis, the Cetims and the Dodanims.
10.5: Of these were divided the Isles of Nations in their countries, each one according to his tongue, & their families in their nations.
10.6: And the sons of Cham: Chus, & Mesraim, & Phut, and Chanaan.
10.7: And the sons of Chus: Saba, Hevila, and Sabatha, & Regma, & Sabathaca. The sons of Regma: Saba, and Dadan.
10.8: Moreover Chus begat Nemrod: he began to be mighty in the earth.
10.9: and he was a valiant hunter before our Lord. Thereof rose a proverb: As it were Nemrod the valiant hunter before our Lord.
10.10: And the beginning of his kingdom was Babylon, and Arach, and Achad, and Chalanne in the land of Sennaar.
10.11: Out of that land came forth Assur, & builded Ninive, & the streets of the city, and Chale.
10.12: Resen also between Ninive, & Chale: this is the great city.
10.13: But Mesraim also begat the Ludims, & the Anamims, & the Laabims, the Nephthuims,
10.14: and the Phetrusims, & the Casluims: of whom came forth the Philistims & the Caphtorims.
10.15: And Chanaan begat Sidon his first-begotten, Hethæus,
10.16: and Iebusæus, and Amorrhæus, and Gergesæus.
10.18: and Aradius, Samaræus, and Hamathæus: and afterwards were spread the people of the Chananites.
10.19: And the limits of Chanaan were from Sidon as we come to Gerara even to Gaza, until thou enter to Sodoma and Gomorrha, and Adama, and Seboim, even to Lesa.
10.20: These are the children of Cham in their kindreds, and tongues, and generations, and lands, and nations.
10.21: Of Sem also father of all the children of Heber, the elder brother of Japheth, were born:
10.22: The children of Sem: Ælam and Assur, and Arphaxad, and Lud, and Aram.
10.23: The children of Aram: Us, and Hul, and Gether, and Mes.
10.24: And Arphaxad also begat Sale, of whom was born Heber.
10.25: And to Heber were born two sons: the name of the one was Phaleg, because that in his days was the earth divided: and his brother's name was Jectan.
10.26: The which Jectan begat Elmodad, and Saleph, and Asarmoth, Jare;
10.27: and Aduram, and Uzal, and Decla,
10.29: and Ophir, and Hevila, and Jobab. All these were the children of Jectan.
10.30: And their dwelling was from Messa as we go on as far as Sephar a mountain in the east.
10.31: These are the children of Sem according to their kindred, and tongues, and countries in their nations.
10.32: These are the families of Noe, according to their peoples and nations. Of these were the nations divided on the earth after the flood.

11.1: And the earth was of one tongue, and all one speech.
11.2: And when they removed from the east, they found a plain in the land of Sennaar, and dwelt in it.
11.3: And each one said to his neighbour: Come, let us make brick, and bake them with fire. And they had brick instead of stone, and bitume instead of mortar:
11.4: and they said, Come, let us make us a city and a tower, the top whereof may reach to heaven: and let us renown our name before we be dispersed into all lands.
11.5: And our Lord descended to see the city and the tower, which the children of Adam builded,
11.6: and he said: Behold, it is one people, and one tongue is to all: and they have begun to do this, neither will they leave off from their determinations, till they accomplish them indeed.
11.7: Come ye therefore, let us go down, and there confound their tongue, that none may hear his neighbour's voice.
11.8: And so our Lord dispersed them from that place into all lands, and they ceased to build the city.
11.9: And therefore the name thereof was called Babel, because there the tongue of the whole earth was confounded: and from thence our Lord dispersed them upon the face of all countries.
11.10: These are the generations of Sem: Sem was an hundred years old when he begat Arphaxad, two years after the flood.
11.11: And Sem lived after he begat Arphaxad, five hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.
11.12: Moreover Arphaxad lived thirty five years, and begat Sale.
11.13: And Arphaxad lived after he begat Sale, three hundred three years, and begat sons and daughters.
11.14: Sale also lived thirty years, and begat Heber.
11.15: And Sale lived after he begat Heber, four hundred three years, & begat sons & daughters.
11.16: And Heber lived thirty four years, and begat Phaleg.
11.17: And Heber lived after he begat Phaleg, four hundred thirty years, and begat sons and daughters.
11.18: Phaleg also lived thirty years, and begat Reu.
11.19: And Phaleg lived after he begat Reu, two hundred nine years, and begat sons and daughters.
11.20: And Reu lived thirty two years, and begat Sarug.
11.21: Reu lived also after he begat Sarug, two hundred seven years, and begat sons and daughters.
11.22: And Sarug lived thirty years, and begat Nachor.
11.23: And Sarug lived after he begat Nachor, two hundred years, and begat sons and daughters.
11.24: And Nachor lived nine and twenty years, and begat Thare.
11.25: And Nachor lived after he begat Thare, and hundred and nineteen years, and begat sons and daughters.
11.26: And Thare lived seventy years, and begat Abram, and Nachor, and Aran.
11.27: And these are the generations of Thare: Thare begat Abram, Nachor, and Aran. Moreover Aran begat Lot.
11.28: And Aran died before Thare his father, in the land of his nativity, in Ur of the Chaldees.
11.29: And Abram and Nachor married wives: the name of Abram his wife was Sarai: and the name of Nachor his wife, Melcha, the daughter of Aran the father of Melcha, and the father of Jescha.
11.30: And Sarai was barren, neither had she children.
11.31: Thare therefore took Abram his son, and Lot the son of Aran, his son's son, and Sarai his daughter in law, the wife of Abram his son, and brought them out of Ur of the Chaldees, for to go into the land of Chanaan: and they came as far as Haran, and dwelled there.
11.32: And the days of Thare came to two hundred five years, and he died in Haran.

12.1: And our Lord said to Abram: Go forth of thy country, and out of thy kindred, and out of thy father's house, and come into a land which I will shew thee.
12.2: And I will make thee into a great nation, and I will bless thee, and magnify thy name, and thou shalt be blessed.
12.3: I will bless them that bless thee, and curse them that curse thee, and IN Thee shall all the kindreds of the earth be blessed.
12.4: Abram therefore went out as our Lord had commanded him, and with him went Lot: seventy five years old was Abraham when he went forth out of Haran.
12.5: And he took Sarai his wife, and Lot his brother's son, and all the substance which they had possessed, and the souls which they had gotten in Haran: and went forth to go into the land of Chanaan. And when they were come into it.
12.6: Abram passed through the country unto the place Sichem, as far as the noble vale: and the Chananite was at that time in the country.
12.7: And our Lord appeared to Abram, and said to him: To thy seed will I give this land. Who builded there an altar to our Lord, that had appeared to him.
12.8: And marching on from thence to a mountain, that was on the east side of Bethel, there he pitched his tent, having Bethel on the west, and Hai on the east: he builded there also an altar to our Lord, and called upon his name.
12.9: And Abram went forward going, and proceeding on to the south.
12.10: And there came a famine in the country: and Abram descended into Ægypt, to be as a pilgrim there: for the famine was very sore in the land.
12.11: And when he was near to enter into Ægypt, he said to Sarai his wife: I know that thou art a fair woman,
12.12: and that when the Ægyptians shall see thee, they will say: She is his wife; and they will kill me, and reserve thee.
12.13: Say therefore, I pray thee, that thou art my sister: that I may be well used for thee, and that my soul may live for thy sake.
12.14: When Abram therefore was entered into Ægypt, the Ægyptians, saw the woman that she was passing beautiful.
12.15: And the Princes told Pharao, and praised her to him: and the woman was taken into the house of Pharao.
12.16: And they used Abram well for her sake. And he had sheep and oxen and he asses, and men servants, and maid servants, and she asses, and Camels.
12.17: And our Lord scourged Pharao with very sore plagues, and his house, for Sarai Abram's wife.
12.18: And Pharao called Abram, and said to him: What is this that thou hast done to me? Why didst thou not tell me that she was thy wife?
12.19: For what cause didst thou say she was thy sister, that I might take her to my wife? Now therefore there is thy wife, take her, and go thy ways.
12.20: And Pharao gave certain men commandment in the behalf of Abram: and they conducted him, and his wife, and all that he had.

13.1: Abram therefore ascended out of Ægypt, he and his wife, and all that he had, and Lot with him to the south coast.
13.2: And he was very rich in possession of gold and silver.
13.3: And he returned by the way that he came, from the south unto Bethel, even to the place where before he had pitched a tabernacle between Bethel and Hai:
13.4: in the place of the altar which he had made before, and there he called upon the name of our Lord.
13.5: But Lot also that was with Abram had flocks of sheep, and herds of beasts, and tents.
13.6: Neither was the land able to receive them for to dwell together: for their substance was much, and they could not dwell together.
13.7: Whereupon also there arose strife amongst the herdsmen of Abram and of Lot. And that time the Chananite and the Pherisite dwelled in that country.
13.8: Abram therefore said to Lot: Let there be no brawl, I beseech thee, between me and thee, and between my herdsmen, and thy herdsmen: for we be brethren.
13.9: Behold the whole land is before thee: go apart from me, I pray thee: if thou wilt go to the left hand, I will take the right: if thou choose the right hand, I will pass to the left.
13.10: Lot therefore lifting up his eyes, saw all the country about Jordan, which was watered throughout before that our Lord subverted Sodome and Gomorre, as the Paradise of our Lord, and like as Ægypt as men come unto Segor.
13.11: And Lot chose unto him the country about Jordan, and he departed from the East: and they were separated either brother from the other.
13.12: Abram dwelt in the land of Chanaan: and Lot abode in the towns that were about Jordan, and dwelt in Sodome.
13.13: And the men of Sodome were very wicked, and sinners before the face of our Lord out of measure.
13.14: And our Lord said to Abram, after that Lot was separated from him: Lift up thine eyes, and look from the place wherein thou now art, to the north and south, to the east and west:
13.15: All the land which thou seest will I give to thee, and to thy seed for ever.
13.16: And I will make thy seed as the dust of the earth: if any man be able to number the dust of the earth, thy seed also shall he be able to number.
13.17: Arise and walk through the land in the length, and in the breadth thereof: for I will give it to thee.
13.18: Abram therefore removing his tent, came, and dwelt beside the vale of Mambre, which is in Hebron: and he builded there an altar to our Lord.

14.1: And it came to pass in that time, that Amraphel the king of Sennaar, and Arioch the king of Pontus, and Chodorlahomor king of the Elamites, and Thadal the king of Nations.
14.2: made war against Barra the king of Sodome, and against Bersa the king of Gomorra, and against Sennaab the king of Adama, and against Semebar the king of Seboim, and against the king of Bala, the same is Segor.
14.3: All these came together into the wood-land vale, which now is the salt sea.
14.4: For they had served Chodorlahomor twelve years, & the thirteenth year they revolted from him.
14.5: Therefore in the fourteenth year came Chodorlahomor, and the kings that were with him, and they struck Raphaim in Astarothcarnaim, and Susim with them, and Emim in Savee of Cariathaim,
14.6: and the Corrheans in the mountains of Seir, even to the Champion country of Pharan, which is in the wilderness.
14.7: And they returned, and came as far as the fountain of Misphat, the same is Cades: and they struck all the country of the Amelechites, and of the Amorrheans, that dwell in Asasonthamar.
14.8: And they went forth the king of Sodome, and the king of Gomorra, and the king of Adama, and the king of Seboim, moreover also the king of Bala, which is Segor: and they set themselves against them in battle array in the wood-land vale:
14.9: to wit against Chodorlahomor king of the Elamites, and Thadal king of Nations, and Amraphel king of Sennaar, and Arioch king of Pontus: four kings against five.
14.10: But the wood-land vale had many pits of bitume. Therefore the king of Sodome and of Gomorra turned their backs, and were overthrown there: and they that remained fled to the mountain.
14.11: And they took all the substance of the Sodomites and Gomorrheans, and took all kind of victuals, and went their way:
14.12: and Lot also and his substance, the son of Abrams brother, who dwelled in Sodom.
14.13: And behold one that had escaped told Abram the Hebrew, that dwelt in the vale of Mambre, the Amorrean, brother of Eschol, and the brother of Aner: for these made a league with Abram.
14.14: Which when Abram had heard, to wit, that his brother Lot was taken, he numbered of the servants born in his house, well appointed, three hundred and eighteen: and pursued them unto Dan.
14.15: And dividing his company, he ran upon them in the night, and struck them, and pursued them unto Hoba, which is on the left hand of Damascus.
14.16: And he brought back all the substance, and Lot his brother with his substance, the women also and the people.
14.17: And the king of Sodom went forth to meet them, after he returned from the slaughter of Chodorlahomor, and of the kings that were with him in the vale Savee, which is the king's vale.
14.18: But Melchisedech the king of Salem, bringing forth bread and wine, for he was the Priest of God most High,
14.19: blessed him, and said: Blessed be Abram to God the Highest, which created heaven and earth:
14.20: and blessed be God the Highest, by whose protection the enemies are in thy hands. And he gave him the tithes of all.
14.21: And the king of Sodom said to Abram: Give me the souls, and the rest take to thee.
14.22: Who answered him: I lift up my hand to my Lord God most High, possessor of heaven and earth,
14.23: that from the very woof-thread unto the shoe latchet, I will not take of all that are thine, lest thou say, I have enriched Abram:
14.24: except such things as the young men have eaten, and the shares of the men that came with me, Aner, Eschol, and Mambre: these shall take their shares.

15.1: When these things therefore were done, the word of our Lord was made to Abram by a vision saying: fear not Abram, I am thy Protector, and thy reward exceeding great.
15.2: And Abram said: Lord God, what wilt thou give me? I shall go without children: and the son of the steward of my house is this Damascus Eliezer.
15.3: And Abram added: But to me thou hast not given seed: and Lo my servant born in my house shall be mine heir.
15.4: And immediately the word of our Lord came to him saying: He shall not be thine heir: but he that shall come out of thy womb him shalt thou have thine heir.
15.5: And he brought him forth abroad, and said to him: Look up to heaven, and number the stars, if thou canst. And he said to him: so shall thy seed be.
15.6: Abram believed God, and it was reputed to him unto justice.
15.7: And he said to him: I am the Lord that brought thee out from Ur of the Chaldees for to give thee this land, and that thou mightest possess it.
15.8: But he said: Lord God, how may I know that I shall possess it?
15.9: And our Lord answered, and said: Take me a cow of three years old, and a she goat of three years, and a ram of three years, a turtle also, and a pigeon.
15.10: Who taking all these, divided them by the midst, and laid each two pieces arow one against the other: but the birds he divided not.
15.11: And the fowls lighted upon the carcasses, and Abram drove them away.
15.12: And when the sun was setting, a deep sleep fell upon Abram, and a great and darksome horror invaded him.
15.13: And it was said unto him: Know and foreknow that a pilgrim shall thy seed be in a land not their own (and they shall bring them under bondage, and afflict them) four hundred years.
15.14: But the Nation, whom they shall serve, I will judge: and after this they shall go forth with great substance.
15.15: And thou shalt go to thy fathers in peace, buried in a good old age.
15.16: But in the fourth generation they shall return hither: for as yet the iniquities of the Amorrheans are not at the full until this present time.
15.17: Therefore when the sun was set, there arose a dark mist, and there appeared a furnace smoking, and a flake of fire passing between those divisions.
15.18: That day God made a covenant with Abram, saying: To thy seed will I give this land from the river of Ægypt even to the great river Euphrates.
15.19: the Cineans, and Cenezeites, the Cedmonites,
15.20: and the Hethits, and the Pherezits, the Raphaims also,
15.21: and the Amorreans and the Chananites, and Gargasites, and the Jebusites.

16.1: Sarai therefore the wife of Abram had brought forth no children; but having an handmaid, an Ægyptian named Agar,
16.2: she said to her husband: Behold, our Lord hath closed me, that I might not bear; Go in unto my handmaid, if happily of her at the least I may have children. And when he agreed to her in this request,
16.3: she took Agar the Ægyptian her handmaid ten years after that they first dwelled in the land of Chanaan, and gave her unto her husband to wife.
16.4: Who did company with her, but she perceiving that she was with child, despised her mistress.
16.5: And Sarai said to Abram: Thou doest unjustly against me: I gave my handmaid into thy bosom, who perceiving herself to be with child, despiseth me. Our Lord judge between me and thee.
16.6: To whom Abram making answer: Behold, saith he, thy handmaid is in thine own hand, use her as it pleaseth thee. When Sarai therefore did afflict her, she ran away.
16.7: And an Angel of our Lord having found her beside a fountain of water in the wilderness, which is in the way to Sur in the desert,
16.8: he said to her: Agar, the handmaid of Sarai, whence comest thou? And whither goest thou? Who answered: From the face of Sarai my mistress do I fly.
16.9: And the Angel of our Lord said to her: Return to thy mistress, and humble thyself under her hand.
16.10: And again: Multiplying, saith he, will I multiply thy seed, and it shall not be numbered for the multitude thereof.
16.11: And again after that: Behold, saith he, thou art with child, and thou shalt bring forth a son; and thou shalt call his name Ismael, because the Lord hath heard thine affliction.
16.12: He shall be a wild man: his hand shall be against all men, and all men's hands against him; and over against all his brethren shall he pitch his tents.
16.13: And she called the name of our Lord that spake unto her: Thou the God which hast seen me. For she said: verily here have I seen the back parts of him that hast seen me.
16.14: Therefore she called that well, the well of him that liveth and seeth me. The same is between Cadesse and Barad.
16.15: And Agar brought forth a son to Abram: Who called his name Ismael.
16.16: Eighty and six years old was Abram when Agar brought him forth Ismael.

17.1: And after that he began to be ninety and nine years old, our Lord appeared unto him, and said unto him: I am the God almighty: walk before me, and be perfect.
17.2: And I will make my covenant between me and thee: and I will multiply thee exceedingly.
17.3: Abram fell flat on his face.
17.4: And God said to him: I am, and my covenant is with thee, and thou shalt be a father of many Nations.
17.5: Neither shall thy name be called any more Abram; but thou shalt be called Abraham: because a father of many Nations I have made thee.
17.6: And I will make thee increase exceedingly, and I will make thee into Nations, and kings shall come forth of thee.
17.7: And I will establish my covenant between me and thee, and between thy seed after thee in their generations by a perpetual covenant: to be thy God, and thy seeds after thee.
17.8: And I will give to thee, and to thy seed the land of thy peregrination, all the land of Chanaan for a perpetual possession, and I will be their God.
17.9: Again God said to Abraham: And thou therefore shalt keep my covenant, and thy seed after thee in their generations.
17.10: This is my covenant which you shall observe between me and you, and thy seed after thee: All the male kind of you shall be circumcised:
17.11: and you shall circumcise the flesh of your prepuce, that it may be for a sign of the covenant between me and you.
17.12: An infant of eight days shall be circumcised among you, all male-kind in your generations: as well the homebred shall be circumcised, as the bought servant of whosoever he is, not of your stock:
17.13: and my covenant shall be in your flesh for a perpetual covenant.
17.14: The male, whose flesh of his prepuce shall not be circumcised, that soul shall be destroyed out of his people: because he hath broken my covenant.
17.15: God said also to Abraham: Sarai thy wife thou shalt not call Sarai, but Sara.
17.16: And I will bless her, and of her I will give thee a son, whom I will bless, and he shall be into Nations, and kings of peoples shall spring of him.
17.17: Abraham fell upon his face, and laughed, saying in his heart: Shall trowest thou to him that is an hundred year old a son be born? And Sara that is ninety years old shall she bear?
17.18: And he said to God: I would that Ismael may live before thee.
17.19: And God said to Abraham: Sara thy wife shall bear thee a son, and thou shalt call his name Isaac, and I will establish my covenant to him for a perpetual covenant, and to his seed after him.
17.20: Concerning Ismael also I have heard thee. Behold, I will bless him, and increase and multiply him exceedingly: twelve Dukes shall he beget, and I will make him into a great Nation.
17.21: But my covenant I will establish with Isaac, whom Sara shall bring forth to thee at this time another year.
17.22: And when he had left off speaking with him, God ascended from Abraham.
17.23: And Abraham took Ismael his son, and all the homebred of his house: and all whom he had bought, all the males of all the men of his house: and he circumcised the flesh of their prepuce forthwith the very same day, as God had commanded him.
17.24: Abraham was ninety and nine years old, when he circumcised the flesh of his prepuce.
17.25: And Ismael his son was full thirteen years old at the time of his circumcision.
17.26: The self-same day was Abraham circumcised and Ismael his son.
17.27: And all the men of his house, as well the homebred as the bought servants and strangers were circumcised together.

18.1: And God appeared to him in the vale of Mambre as he sat in the door of his tent, in the very heat of the day.
18.2: And when he had lifted up his eyes, there appeared to him three men standing near unto him: whom after he had seen, he ran to meet them from the door of his tent, and adored to the ground.
18.3: And He said Lord, if I have found grace in thy sight, go not past thy servant:
18.4: but I will fetch a little water, and wash ye your feet, and rest ye under the tree.
18.5: And I will set a morsel of bread, and strengthen your heart, afterward you shall pass: for therefore are you come aside to your servant. Who said: Do as thou hast spoken.
18.6: Abraham made haste into the tent to Sara, and said to her: Make haste, temper together three measures of flour, and make hearth cakes.
18.7: But himself ran to the herd, and took from thence a calf very tender and very good, and gave it to a young man: who made haste and boiled it.
18.8: He took also butter and milk, and the calf which he had boiled, and set before them: but himself did stand beside them under the tree.
18.9: And when they had eaten, they said to him: Where is Sara thy wife? He answered: Lo she is in the tent.
18.10: To whom he said: Returning I will come to thee at this time, life accompanying, and Sara thy wife shall have a son . Which when Sara heard, she laughed behind the door of the tent.
18.11: And they were both aged, and far entered in years, and it ceased to be with Sara after the manner of women.
18.12: Who laughed secretly saying: After I am waxen old, and my Lord is an old one, shall I give myself to pleasure?
18.13: And our Lord said to Abraham: Why did Sara laugh, saying: Shall I an old woman bear a child indeed?
18.14: Is there any thing hard to God? According to appointment I will return to thee this very self-same time, life accompanying, & Sara shall have a son.
18.15: Sara denied, saying: I laughed not; being much afraid. But our Lord: It is not so, saith he, but thou didst laugh.
18.16: When the men therefore were risen up from thence, they turned their eyes against Sodome: and Abraham did go with them, bringing them on the way.
18.17: And our Lord said: Can I conceal from Abraham the things which I will do,
18.18: whereas he shall be into a Nation great, and very strong, and in him are TO BE Blessed All The Nations of The Earth?
18.19: For I know that he will command his children, and his house after him that they keep the way of the Lord, & do judgement and justice: that for Abraham's sake the Lord may bring to effect all the things that he hath spoken unto him. Therefore said our Lord:
18.20: The cry of Sodome and Gomorre is multiplied, and their sin is aggravated exceedingly.
18.21: I will descend, and see whether they have in act accomplished the cry that is come to me: or whether it be not so, that I may know.
18.22: And they turned themselves from thence, and went their way to Sodome: but Abraham as yet stood before our Lord.
18.23: And approaching he said: What! Wilt thou destroy the just with the wicked?
18.24: If there shall be fifty just persons in the city, shall they perish withal? And wilt thou spare that place for fifty just, if they be therein?
18.25: Be it far from thee, that thou do this thing, and that thou kill the just with the wicked, and that the just be in like case as the wicked, this is not beseeming thee, which judgest all the earth: no, thou wilt not do this judgement.
18.26: And our Lord said to him: If I shall find in Sodome fifty just persons within the city, I will spare the whole place for their sake.
18.27: And Abraham answered, and said: Because I have once begun, I will speak to my Lord, whereas I am dust and ashes.
18.28: What if there shall be five less than fifty just persons? Wilt thou for forty five destroy the whole city? And he said: I will not destroy it, if I shall find five and forty.
18.29: And again he said unto him: But if forty shall be found there, what wilt thou do? He said: I will not strike it for forty's sake.
18.30: Lord, saith he, be not angry I beseech thee, if I speak: What if thirty shall be found there? He answered: I will not do it, if I shall find thirty there.
18.31: Because, saith he, I have once begun, I will speak to my Lord: What if twenty shall be found there? He said: I will not destroy it for twenty's sake.
18.32: I beseech thee, saith he, be not angry Lord, if I speak yet once more: What if ten shall be found there? And he said: I will not destroy it for ten's sake.
18.33: And our Lord departed after that he ceased to speak unto Abraham: and Abraham returned into his place.

19.1: And the two Angels came to Sodome at even, and Lot sitting in the gates of the city. Who when he had seen them, rose up & went to meet them: and adored prostrate unto the ground,
19.2: and said: I beseech you, my Lords, turn into the house of your servant, and lodge there: Wash your feet, and in the morning you shall go forth on your way. Who said: No, but we will abide in the street.
19.3: He compelled them earnestly to turn in unto him: and when they were entered into his house, he made them a banquet, and baked unleavened bread, and they did eat.
19.4: And before they went to bed, the men of the city beset the house from young to old, all the people together.
19.5: And they called Lot, and said to him: Where are the men that came in to thee at night? Bring them forth hither that we may know them.
19.6: Lot going forth to them, and shutting the door after him, said:
19.7: Do not so, I beseech you, my brethren, do not commit this evil.
19.8: I have two daughters, which as yet have not known man: I will bring them forth to you, and abuse you them as it shall please you, so that you do no evil to these men, because they are entered under the shadow of my roof.
19.9: But they said: Get thee back thither. And again: Thou camest in, said they, as a stranger, what to be a Judge? Thyself therefore we will afflict more than these. And they did violence to Lot exceedingly: and it was even now at the point that they would break the doors.
19.10: And behold the men put forth their hand, and drew in Lot unto them, and shut the door:
19.11: and them that were without they struck with blindness from the least to the greatest, so that they could not find the door.
19.12: And they said to Lot: Hast thou here any of thine? Son in law, or sons, or daughters, all that are thine, bring them out of this city:
19.13: for we will destroy this place, for that their cry is waxen loud before our Lord, who hath sent us to destroy them.
19.14: Therefore Lot went forth, and spake to his sons in law that were to take his daughters, and said: Arise, get you forth out of this place, because our Lord will destroy this city. And he seemed unto them to speak as it were in jest.
19.15: And when it was morning, the Angels urged him, saying: Arise, take thy wife, and the two daughters which thou hast: lest thou also perish withal in the wickedness of the city.
19.16: He lingering, they took his hand, and the hand of his wife, and of his two daughters, because our Lord spared him.
19.17: And they led him forth, and set him without the city; and there they spake to him, saying: Save thy life; look not back, neither stay thou in all the country about: but save thyself in the mountain, lest thou also perish withal.
19.18: And Lot said to them: I beseech thee my Lord,
19.19: because thy servant hath found grace before thee, and thou hast magnified thy mercy, which thou hast wrought with me, in that thou wouldest save my life, and safe I cannot be in the mountain, lest perhaps the evil catch me, and I die.
19.20: There is this city here-by at hand, whereunto I may fly, a little one, and I shall be safe in it: is it not a little one, and my life shall be saved?
19.21: And he said to him: Behold also in this point I have heard thy prayers, not to overthrow the city for which thou hast spoken.
19.22: Make haste, and be saved there: because I cannot do any thing till thou enter in thither. Therefore the name of that city was called Segor.
19.23: The sun was risen upon the earth, and Lot entered into Segor.
19.24: Therefore our Lord rained upon Sodome and Gomorre brimstone & fire from our Lord out of heaven:
19.25: and he subverted these cities, and all the country about, all the inhabitants of the cities, and all things that spring of the earth.
19.26: And his wife looking behind her, was turned into a statua of salt.
19.27: And Abraham getting up early in the morning, there where before he had stood with our Lord,
19.28: beheld Sodome and Gomorre, and the whole land of that country: and he saw the cinders rise up from the earth as it were the smoke of a furnace.
19.29: For when God subverted the cities of that country, he remembering Abraham, delivered Lot out of the subversion of the cities wherein he had dwelt.
19.30: And Lot ascended out of Segor, and abode in the mountain, his two daughters also with him (for he was afraid to abide in Segor) and he abode in a cave himself, and his two daughters with him.
19.31: And the elder said to the younger: Our father is old, and there is no man left on the earth, that may company with us after the manner of the whole earth.
19.32: Come, let us make him drunk with wine, and let us lie with him, that we may preserve seed of our father.
19.33: They therefore made their father to drink wine that night: and the elder went in, and lay with her father: but he perceived not, neither when his daughter lay down, nor when she rose up.
19.34: The next day also the elder said to the younger: Behold I lay yesternight with my father, let us make him drink wine also this night, and thou shalt lie with him, that we may save seed of our father.
19.35: They made their father drink wine that night also, and the younger daughter went in, and lay with him: and neither then truly did he perceive when she lay down, or when she rose up.
19.36: The two daughters therefore of Lot were with child by their father.
19.37: And the elder bare a son and she called his name Moab: he is the father of the Moabites even to this present day.
19.38: The younger also bare a son, and she called his name Ammon, that is the son of my people: he is the father of the Ammonites even to this day.

20.1: Abraham removed from thence into the south country and dwelt between Cades, and Sur: and he lived as a pilgrim in Gerara.
20.2: And he said of Sara his wife: She is my sister. Abimelech therefore, the king of Gerara, sent and took her.
20.3: And God came to Abimelech in a dream by night, and said to him: Lo thou shalt die for the woman that thou hast taken: for she hath a husband.
20.4: But Abimelech had not touched her, and he said: Lord wilt thou kill a Nation that is ignorant & just?
20.5: Did not he say to me: She is my sister? And she say, He is my brother? In simplicity of my heart and cleanness of my hands have I done this.
20.6: And God said to him: And I do know that thou didst it with a sincere heart: and therefore I kept thee that thou shouldest not sin against me, and I permitted not that thou shouldest touch her.
20.7: Now therefore restore the wife to her husband, because he is a Prophet: and he shall pray for thee, and thou shalt live. But if thou wilt not restore her, know thou that dying thou shalt die, thou and all things that are thine.
20.8: And Abimelech forthwith rising up in the night, called all his servants: and he spake all these words to their ears, and all the men were sore afraid.
20.9: And Abimelech called also for Abraham, & said to him: What hast thou done to us? What have we offended against thee, that thou hast brought upon me and upon my kingdom a great sin? That which thou oughtest not to do, thou hast done to us.
20.10: And again expostulating, he said: What sawest thou, that thou didest this?
20.11: Abraham answered: I thought with myself, saying: Perhaps there is not the fear of God in this place, & they will kill me for my wife:
20.12: howbeit otherwise also in very deed she is my sister, the daughter of my father, & not the daughter of my mother, & I took her to wife.
20.13: And after that God brought me out of my father's house, I said to her: This mercy thou shalt do with me: In every place which we shall come unto, thou shalt say that I am thy brother.
20.14: Therefore Abimelech took sheep and oxen, and servants, and handmaids, and gave to Abraham: and restored to him Sara his wife,
20.15: and said: The land is before you, dwell wheresoever it shalt please thee.
20.16: And to Sara he said: Behold I have given thy brother a thousand pieces of silver, this shall serve thee for a veil of thine eyes to all that are with thee, and whithersoever thou shalt go, remember also thou wast taken.
20.17: And Abraham praying, God healed Abimelech and his wife, and his handmaids, and they bare children:
20.18: for our Lord had closed up every matrice of the house of Abimelech for Sara Abraham's wife.

21.1: And our Lord visited Sara, as he had promised: and fulfilled the things which he spake.
21.2: And she conceived & bare a son in her old age, at the time that God had foretold her.
21.3: And Abraham called the name of his son, which Sara bare him, Isaac:
21.4: and he circumcised him the eighth day, as God had commanded him,
21.5: when he was an hundred years old: for at this age of his father, was Isaac born.
21.6: And Sara said: Laughter God hath made to me: whosoever shall hear of it, will laugh with me.
21.7: And again she said: Who that shall hear of it would believe Abraham, that Sara gave suck to a son, which she bare him now being an old man?
21.8: The child therefore grew and was weaned: and Abraham made a great feast in the day of his weaning.
21.9: And when Sara had seen the son of Agar the Ægyptian playing with Isaac her son, she said to Abraham:
21.10: Cast out this handmaid, & her son: for the son of the handmaid shall not be heir with my son Isaac.
21.11: Abraham took this grievously for his son.
21.12: To whom God said: Let it not seem grievous to thee for the boy, and for thy handmaid: all things that Sara shall say to thee, hear her voice: because in Isaac shall seed be called to thee.
21.13: But the son also of the handmaid I will make into a great nation, because he is thy seed.
21.14: Abraham therefore rose up in the morning, and taking bread and a bottle of water, put it upon her shoulder, and delivered the boy, and dismissed her. Who went away, and wandered in the wilderness of Bersabee.
21.15: And when the water in the bottle was spent, she cast the boy under one of the trees that were there.
21.16: And she went her way, and sat over against a great way off as far as a bow can cast, for she said: I will no see the child dying. And sitting over against, she lifted up her voice and wept.
21.17: And God heard the voice of the boy: and an Angel of God called Agar from heaven, saying: What doest thou Agar? Fear not; for God hath heard the voice of the boy, from the place wherein he is.
21.18: Arise, take up the boy, & hold his hand; for into a great nation will I make him.
21.19: And God opened her eyes: who seeing a well of water, went, and filled the bottle, and gave the boy to drink.
21.20: And God was with him: who grew, and abode in the wilderness, and became a young man archer.
21.21: And he dwelt in the wilderness of Pharan, and his mother took a wife for him out of the land of Ægypt.
21.22: The same time said Abimelech, and Phicol the General of his army, to Abraham: God is with thee in all things which thou doest.
21.23: Swear therefore by God, not to hurt me, and my posterity, and my stock: but according to the mercy, that I have done thee, thou shalt do to me, and to the land wherein thou hast lived a stranger.
21.24: And Abraham said: I will swear.
21.25: And he rebuked Abimelech for the well of water, which his servants had taken away by force.
21.26: And Abimelech answered: I knew not who did this thing: yea and thyself didst not tell me, and I heard not of it but today.
21.27: Abraham therefore took sheep and oxen, and gave to Abimelech: and both of them made a league.
21.28: And Abraham set seven ewe lambs of the flock apart.
21.29: To whom Abimelech said: What mean these seven ewe lambs, which thou hast made to stand apart?
21.30: But he said: Seven ewe lambs shalt thou take at my hand: that they may be a testimony for me, that I digged this well.
21.31: Therefore was that place called Bersabee: because there both did swear.
21.32: And they made a league for the well of oath.
21.33: And Abimelech arose, and Phicol the General of his army, and they returned to the land of the Palestines. But Abraham planted a grove in Bersabee, and called there upon the name of our Lord God eternal.
21.34: And he was a sojourner in the land of the Palestines many days.

22.1: Which things being done, God tempted Abraham, and said to him: Abraham, Abraham. But he answered: Here I am.
22.2: He said to him: Take thy only begotten son, whom thou lovest, Isaac, and go into the Land of vision: and there thou shalt offer him for an holocaust upon one of the mountains which I will shew thee.
22.3: Therefore Abraham rising up in the night, saddled his ass: taking with him two young men, and Isaac his son: and when he had cut wood for the holocaust, he went his way to the place which God had commanded him.
22.4: And the third day, lifting up his eyes, he saw the place a far off:
22.5: and he said to his young men: Tarry you here with the ass: I and the boy going with speed as far as yonder, after we have adored will return to you.
22.6: He took also the wood of the holocaust, and laid it upon Isaac his son: and himself carried in his hand fire and a sword. And as they went on together,
22.7: Isaac said to his father: My father. And he answered: What wilt thou son? Behold, saith he, fire & wood; where is the victim of the holocaust?
22.8: And Abraham said: God will provide unto himself the victim of the holocaust, my son. They went on therefore together:
22.9: and they came to the place which God had shewed him, wherein he builded an altar, and laid the wood in order upon it: and when he had bound Isaac his son, he laid him on the altar upon the pile of wood.
22.10: And he stretched forth his hand, and caught the sword, for to sacrifice his son.
22.11: And behold an Angel of our Lord from heaven cried, saying: Abraham, Abraham. Who answered: Here I am.
22.12: And he said to him: Stretch not forth thy hand upon the boy, neither do thou any thing to him: now have I known that thou fearest God, and hast not spared thine only begotten son for my sake.
22.13: Abraham lifted up his eyes, and saw behind his back a ram amongst the briers sticking fast by the horns, which he took & offered an holocaust instead of his son.
22.14: And he called the name of that place, Our Lord seeth. Whereupon even to this day it is said, In the mountain our Lord will see.
22.15: And the Angel of our Lord called Abraham the second time from heaven, saying:
22.16: By my own self have I sworn, saith the Lord: because thou hast done this thing, and hast not spared thine only begotten son for my sake;
22.17: I will bless thee, and I will multiply thy seed as the stars of heaven, and as the sand that is by the sea shore: thy seed shall possess the gates of his enemies,
22.18: and IN Thy Seed Shall be Blessed All The Nations of The Earth, because thou hast obeyed my voice.
22.19: Abraham returned to his young men, and they went to Bersabee together, and he dwelt there.
22.20: These things so being done, it was told Abraham that Melcha also had born children to Nachor his brother,
22.21: Hus the first-begotten, and Buz his brother, and Camuel the father of the Sirians,
22.22: and Cased, and Azau, Pheldas also and Iedlaph,
22.23: and Bathuel, of whom was born Rebecca: these eight did Melcha bear to Nachor Abraham's brother.
22.24: And his concubine, named Roma, bare Tabee, and Gaham, and Tahas, and Maacha.

23.1: And Sara lived an hundred twenty seven years.
23.2: And she died in the city of Arbee which is Hebron, in the land of Chanaan: And Abraham came to mourn and weep for her.
23.3: And after that he was risen up from the funeral obsequies, he spake to the children of Heth, saying:
23.4: I am a stranger and pilgrim among you: give me the right of a sepulchre with you, that I may bury my dead.
23.5: The children of Heth answered, saying:
23.6: My lord hear us, thou art a prince of God among us: in our principal sepulchres bury thou thy dead: and no man can let thee but that in his own monument thou mayest bury thy dead.
23.7: Abraham rose up and adored the people of the land, to wit the children of Heth;
23.8: and he said to them: If it please your soul that I bury my dead, hear me, and be intercessors for me to Ephron the son of Seor:
23.9: that he give me the double cave, which he hath in the uttermost part of his field: for money to the worth therefore let him deliver it to me before you for possession of a sepulchre.
23.10: And Ephron dwelt in the midst of the children of Heth. And Ephron made answer to Abraham in the hearing of all that went in at the gate of the city, saying:
23.11: No, it shall not be so, my lord, but thou rather hearken to that which I do say: The field I deliver to thee, and the cave that is therein, in the presence of the children of my people, bury thy dead.
23.12: Abraham adored before the people of the land.
23.13: And he spoke to Ephron, his people standing round about: I beseech thee to hear me: I will give money for the field: take it, and so I will bury my dead in it.
23.15: My lord hear me. The ground which thou desirest is worth four hundred sicles of silver: this is the price between me and thee: but how much is this? Bury thy dead.
23.16: Which when Abraham had heard, he weighed the money that Ephron had asked, in the hearing of the children of Heth, four hundred sicles of silver of common current money.
23.17: And the field that before time was Ephron's, wherein was the double cave, looking towards Mambre, as well it, as the cave, and all the trees thereof in all the limits thereof round about,
23.18: was made sure to Abraham for a possession, in the sight of the children of Heth, and of all that went in at the gate of his city.
23.19: And so Abraham buried Sara his wife in the double cave of the field, that looked towards Mambre, this is Hebron in the land of Chanaan.
23.20: And the field was made sure to Abraham, and the cave that was in it, for a possession to bury in, of the Children of Heth.

24.1: And Abraham was old, and of many days, and our Lord had blessed him in all things.
24.2: And he said to the elder servant of his house, which was ruler over all that he had: Put thy hand under my thigh,
24.3: that I may adjure thee by our Lord God of heaven and earth, that thou take not a wife for my son of the daughters of the Chananites, among whom I dwell:
24.4: but that thou go unto mine own country and kindred, and thence take a wife for my son Isaac.
24.5: The servant answered: If the woman will not come with me into this land, whether must I bring thy son back again to the place, from whence thou didst come forth?
24.6: And Abraham said: Beware thou never bring my son back again thither.
24.7: Our Lord God of heaven, which took me out of my father's house, and out of my native country, which spake to me, and sware to me, saying: To thy seed will I give this land; he shall send his Angel before thee, and thou shall take from thence a wife for my son:
24.8: but if the woman will not follow thee, thou shalt not be bound by the oath: only bring not my son thither again.
24.9: The servant therefore put his hand under the thigh of Abraham his lord, and sware to him upon this word.
24.10: And he took ten camels of his lord's herd, and departed, of all his goods carrying something with him, and setting forward went on into Mesopotamia to the city of Nachor.
24.11: And when he had made the camels lie down without the town beside a well of water at even, at the time when women are wont to come forth to draw water, he said:
24.12: O Lord God of my lord Abraham, meet me today, I beseech thee, and do mercifully with my master Abraham.
24.13: Behold I stand nigh to the fountain of water and the daughters of the inhabiters of this city will come forth to draw water.
24.14: Therefore the maid, to whom I shall say: Bow down thy tankard that I may drink; and she shall answer: Drink, yea to thy camels also will I give drink; she it is, whom thou hast provided for thy servant Isaac: and by this I shall understand, that thou hast dealt mercifully with my master.
24.15: Neither had he yet ended these words within himself, & behold Rebecca came forth, the daughter of Bathuel, the son of Melcha, wife to Nachor the brother of Abraham, having a tankard on her shoulder;
24.16: a passing comely maid, & most beautiful virgin, & not known to man: and she was gone down to the fountain, and had filled her tankard, and came back.
24.17: And the servant ran to meet her, and said: Give me a little water to drink of thy tankard.
24.18: Who answered: Drink my lord. And quickly she let down the tankard upon her arm, and gave him drink.
24.19: And when he had drunk, she added: but for thy camels also I will draw water, till all do drink.
24.20: And pouring out the tankard into the troughs, she ran back to the well to draw water: and being drawn gave it to all the camels.
24.21: But he musing beheld her with silence, desirous to know whether our Lord had made his journey prosperous or not.
24.22: And after that the camels had drunk, the man plucked forth golden earlets, weighing two sicles, and as many bracelets of ten sicles weight.
24.23: And he said to her: Whose daughter art thou? Shew me; is there any place in thy father's house to lodge?
24.24: Who answered: I am the daughter of Bathuel, the son of Melcha, whom she bare to Nachor.
24.25: And she added, saying: Of straw also and hay we have good store, and a large place to lodge in.
24.26: The man bowed himself, and adored our Lord,
24.27: saying: blessed be the Lord God of my lord Abraham, that hath not taken away his mercy & truth from my lord, and hath brought me the straight way into the house of my lord's brother.
24.28: The maid therefore ran, and reported into her mother's house all things that she had heard.
24.29: And Rebecca had a brother named Laban, who in all haste went forth to the man, where the fountain was.
24.30: And when he had seen the earlets and bracelets in his sister's hands, and hand heard all her words reporting: These words spake the man unto me; he came to the man which stood beside the camels, and nigh to the fountain of water:
24.31: and said to him: Come in, thou blessed of our Lord: Why standest thou without? I have prepared the house, and a place for the camels.
24.32: And he brought him into his lodging: and he unharnessed the camels, and gave straw and hay, and water to wash his feet, and of the men that were come with him.
24.33: And bread was set before him. Who said: I will not eat till I speak my message. He answered him: Speak.
24.34: And he said: I am the servant of Abraham:
24.35: And our Lord hath blessed my lord wonderfully, and he is magnified: and he hath given him sheep, & oxen, silver and gold, men servants & women servants, camels, and asses.
