41.55: The which being in hunger, the people cried to Pharao, desiring food. To whom he answered: Go ye to Joseph: & whatsoever he shall say to you, that do ye.
41.56: And the famine daily increased in all the land: and Joseph opened all the barns, and sold to the Ægyptians: for them also the famine had oppressed.
41.57: And all Provinces came into Ægypt, to buy victuals, and to moderate the misery of the scarcity.

42.1: And Jacob hearing that victuals were sold in Ægypt, he said to his sons: Why neglect ye?
42.2: I have heard that wheat is sold in Ægypt: Go ye down and buy us necessaries, that we may live and not be consumed with lack.
42.3: Therefore the ten brethren of Joseph going down to buy corn in Ægypt,
42.4: Benjamin being kept at home by Jacob, who said to his brethren: Lest perhaps he take any harm in the journey:
42.5: entered into the land of Ægypt with others that went to buy. For the famine was in the land of Chanaan.
42.6: And Joseph was Prince in the land of Ægypt, and at his pleasure corn was sold to the people. And when his brethren had adored him,
42.7: and he knew them, he spake as it were to strangers somewhat roughly, asking them: From whence came you? Who answered: From the Land of Chanaan, that we may buy necessaries to live.
42.8: And yet himself knowing his brethren, was not known of them.
42.9: And remembering the dreams, which sometimes he had seen, he said to them: You are spies: to view the weaker parts of the land you are come.
42.10: Who said: It is not so, my Lord, but thy servants are come to buy victuals.
42.11: We are all the sons of one man: we are come as men of peace, neither do thy servants go about any evil.
42.12: To whom he answered: It is otherwise: you came to consider the undefensed parts of this land.
42.13: But they: We thy servants, say they, are twelve brethren, the sons of one man in the Land of Chanaan: the youngest is with our father, the other is not living.
42.14: This is it, quoth he, that I said: You are spies.
42.15: Now presently I will take a trial of you: by the health of Pharao you shall not depart hence, until your youngest brother do come,
42.16: Send you one of you to bring him: and you shall be in prison till the things be proved which you have said, whether they be true or false: otherwise by the health of Pharao you are spies.
42.17: Therefore he put them in prison three days.
42.18: And the third day bringing them out of prison, he said: Do as I have said, and you shall live: for I fear God.
42.19: If you be men of peace, let one of your brethren be bound in prison: and go ye your ways, and carry the corn that you have bought unto your houses,
42.20: and bring your youngest brother to me, that I may prove your sayings to be true, and that you die not. They did as he had said,
42.21: and they talked one to another: Worthily do we suffer these things, because we have sinned against our brother, seeing the distress of his soul whilst he besought us, and we heard not: therefore is this tribulation come upon us.
42.22: Among whom Ruben one of them, said: Why, did not I say to you: Sin not against the boy; and you heard me not? Lo his blood is required.
42.23: And they knew not that Joseph understood: because he spake to them by an interpreter.
42.24: And he turned away himself a little while, and wept: and returning he spake to them.
42.25: And taking Simeon, and binding him in their presence, he commanded his servants that they should fill up their sacks with wheat, and put every man's money again in their bags, giving them besides for to eat on the way: who did so.
42.26: But they carrying their corn upon their asses, took their journey.
42.27: And one of them opening his sack to give his beast provender in the Inn, beholding the money in the sack's mouth,
42.28: he said to his brethren: My money is given me again, behold it is in the sack. And being astonished and troubled amongst themselves, they said: What is this, that God hath done unto us?
42.29: And they came to Jacob their father into the land of Chanaan, & they told him all things that had chanced unto them, saying:
42.30: The lord of the land spake to us roughly, & thought us to be spies of the province,
42.31: to whom we answered: We are men of peace, neither do we attempt any treachery.
42.32: We are twelve brethren born of one father: one is not living, the youngest is with our father in the Land of Chanaan.
42.33: Who said to us: Thus shall I try that you be men of peace: Leave one of your brethren with me, and take ye provision necessary for your houses, and go your ways,
42.34: and bring your youngest brother to me, that I may know you are not spies: and you may receive this fellow again, that is kept in prison: and afterwards may have license to buy what things you will.
42.35: This being said when they poured out their corn, every man found his money tied in the mouth of the sacks: and all being astonished together,
42.36: their father Jacob said: You have made me to be without children, Joseph is not living, Simeon is kept in bonds, and Benjamin you will take away: all these evils, are fallen upon me.
42.37: To whom Ruben answered: Kill my two sons, if I bring him not again to thee: deliver him into my hand, and I will restore him unto thee.
42.38: But he said: My son shall not go down with you: his brother is dead, and he alone is remaining: if any adversity shall chance to him in the land to the which you go, you shall bring down my hoar hairs with sorrow unto hell.

43.1: In the mean time the famine did oppress all the land very sore.
43.2: And the provision being spent, which they had brought out of Ægypt, Jacob said to his sons: Go again, and buy us a little victual.
43.3: Judas answered: That Same man denounced unto us under attestation of an oath, saying: You shall not see my face, unless you bring your youngest brother with you.
43.4: If therefore thou wilt send him with us, we will go forward together, and will buy necessaries for thee:
43.5: but if thou wilt not, we will not go: for the man, as we have often said, denounced unto us saying: You shall not see my face without your youngest brother.
43.6: Israel said to them: You have done this to my misery, in that you told him that you had another brother also.
43.7: But they answered: The man asked us in order our progeny: if our father lived; if we had a brother: and we answered him consequently to that which he demanded: could we know that he would say: Bring hither your brother with you?
43.8: Judas also said to his father: Send the boy with me, that we may set forward, and may live: lest we and our little ones die.
43.9: Itake upon me the boy: require him of my hand, unless I bring him again, and restore him to thee, I will be guilty of sin against thee for ever.
43.10: If delay had not been made, we had been come now the second time.
43.11: Therefore Israel their father said to them: If it must needs be so, do that you will: take of the best fruits of the land in your vessels, and carry to the man for presents, a courtesy of rosen, & of honey, & of incense, of myrrh, of terebinth, and of almonds.
43.12: Double money also carry with you: and recarry that you found in your sacks, lest perhaps it was done by an error:
43.13: but take also your brother, and go to the man.
43.14: And my God almighty make him favourable unto you; and send back with you your brother, whom he keepeth, and this Benjamin: as for me I shall be desolate without children.
43.15: The men therefore took the presents, & the double money, & Benjamin; & went down into Ægypt, & stood before Joseph.
43.16: Whom when he had seen & Benjamin withal, he commanded the steward of his house, saying: Bring in the men into the house, and kill victims, and prepare a feast; because they shall eat with me at noon.
43.17: He did that which was commanded him, and brought the men into the house.
43.18: And there being sore afraid, they said one to another: because of the money which we carried back the first time in our sacks, we are brought in: that he may turn upon us a false accusation, and forcibly bring both us and our asses into bondage.
43.19: Wherefore in the very door stepping to the steward of the house,
43.20: They spake: Sir, we desire thee to hear us. Now once before we came down to buy provision:
43.21: Which being bought, when we were come to the Inn, we opened our sacks, and found our money in the mouths of the sacks: which we have now brought again in the same weight.
43.22: But we have brought other money besides, to buy the things that are necessary for us: our conscience is not privy, who put it in our bags.
43.23: But he answered: Peace be with you, fear you not: your God, and the God of your father hath given you treasures in your sacks. For the money, which you gave me, I have for good. And he brought forth Simeon unto them.
43.24: And being brought into the house, he fetched water, and they washed their feet, and he gave provender to their asses.
43.25: But they made ready the presents, till Joseph should come in at noon: for they had heard that they should eat bread there.
43.26: Therefore Joseph came in to his house, and they offered him presents holding them in their hands, and they adored prostrate to the ground.
43.27: But he courteously resaluting them, asked them saying: Is the old man your father in health, of whom you told me? Is he yet living.
43.28: Who answered: Thy servant our father is in health, he is yet living. And bowing themselves they adored him.
43.29: And Joseph lifting up his eyes, saw Benjamin his brother of the same mother, and said: is this your young brother, of whom you told me? And again: God, saith he, be merciful unto thee my son.
43.30: And he made haste because his heart was moved upon his brother, and tears brake forth: and entering into his chamber he wept.
43.31: And when he had washed his face, coming forth again, he refrained himself, and said: Set bread on the table.
43.32: Which being set down, to Joseph apart, and to his brethren apart, to the Ægyptians also that did eat with him apart (for it is unlawful for the Ægyptians to eat with the Hebrews, and they think such a feast profane)
43.33: they sat before him, the first begotten according to his first-birth, and the youngest according to his age. And they marveled out of measure,
43.34: Taking the portions that they received of him: and the greater portion came to Benjamin, so that it exceeded by five parts. And they drank and were inebriated with him.

44.1: And Joseph commanded the steward of his house, saying: Fill their sacks with corn, as much as they can hold: and put the money of every one in the top of his sack.
44.2: And in the sack's mouth of the younger put my silver cup, and the price which he gave for the wheat. And it was so done.
44.3: And when the morning rose, they were dismissed with their asses.
44.4: And they were now departed out of the city, and had gone forward a little way: then Joseph sending for the steward of his house, said: Arise, quote he, and pursue the men: and overtaking them say to them: Why have you rendered evil for good?
44.5: The cup which you have stolen is that wherein my lord doth drink, and wherein he is wont to divine: you have done a very evil thing.
44.6: He did as he had commanded. And having overtaken them, he spake to them in the same order.
44.7: Who answered: Why doth our lord speak so, as though thy servants had committed so heinous a fact?
44.8: The money that we found in the top of our sacks, we recarried to thee from the land of Chanaan: & how followeth it, that we have stolen out of thy lord's house gold or silver?
44.9: With whom soever of thy servants that shall be found which thou seekest, let him die, and we will be the bondmen of our lord.
44.10: Who said to them: Be it done according to your sentence: with whom soever it shall be found, be he my bondman, and you shall be guiltless.
44.11: Therefore in haste taking down their sacks upon the ground, every man opened.
44.12: Which when he had searched, beginning from the elder even to the youngest, he found the cup in Benjamin's sack.
44.13: But they renting their garments, and loading their asses again, returned into the town.
44.14: And Judas foremost with his brethren entered in to Joseph (for he was not yet gone out of the place) and they fell together before him on the ground.
44.15: To whom he said: Why would you do so? Know you not that there is not the like to me in the science of divining.
44.16: To whom Judas said: What shall we answer, my lord? Or what shall we speak, or be able justly to pretend? God hath found the iniquity of thy servants: lo we are all bondmen to my Lord, both we, & he with whom the cup was found.
44.17: Joseph answered: God forbid that I should so do: he that stole the cup, the same be my bondman: and go you free unto your father.
44.18: And Judas approaching nearer said boldly: I beseech thee my lord, let thy servant speak a word in thine ears, and be not angry with thy servant: for after Pharao thou art,
44.19: my Lord. Thou didst ask thy servants the first time: Have you a father or a brother?
44.20: and we answered thee my lord: We have a father an old man, and a little boy, that was born in his old age; whose brother by the mother is dead: and his mother hath him only, and his father loveth him tenderly.
44.21: And thou saidst to thy servants: Bring him hither to me, and I will set mine eyes on him.
44.22: We suggested to my lord: The boy cannot leave his father: for if he leave him, he will die.
44.23: And thou saidst to thy servants: Unless your youngest brother come with you, you shall no more see my face.
44.24: Therefore when we were gone up to thy servant our father, we told him all things that my lord did speak.
44.25: And our father said: Go again, and buy us a little wheat.
44.26: To whom we said: We cannot go: if our youngest brother shall go down with us, we will set forward together: otherwise he being absent, we dare not see the man's face.
44.27: Whereunto he answered: You know that my wife bare me two.
44.28: One went forth, and you said: A beast did devour him: and hitherto he appeareth not.
44.29: If you take this also, and ought befall him in the way, you shall bring down my hoar hairs with sorrow unto hell.
44.30: Therefore if I shall enter to thy servant our father, and the boy be wanting (whereas his life dependeth upon the life of him)
44.31: and he shall see that he is not with us, he will die, and thy servants shall bring down his hoar hairs with sorrow unto hell.
44.32: Let me be thy proper servant that did take him into my protection and promised, saying: Unless I bring him again I will be guilty of sin against my father for ever.
44.33: Itherefore thy servant will tarry instead of the child in the service of my lord, and let the child go up with his brethren.
44.34: For I cannot return to my father, the child being absent; lest I stand by a witness of the calamity that shall oppress my father.

45.1: Joseph could no longer refrain many standing by in presence: whereupon he commanded that all should go forth, and no stranger should be present at their acknowledging one of another.
45.2: And he lifted up his voice with weeping, which the Ægyptians heard, and all the house of Pharao.
45.3: And he said to his Brethren: I am Joseph: is my father yet living? His brethren could not answer him being terribly astonished out of measure.
45.4: To whom gently he said: Come hither to me. And when they were come near him, I am, quoth he, Joseph your brother, whom you sold into Ægypt.
45.5: Be not afraid, neither let it seem to you a hard case that you did sell me into these countries: for God sent me before you into Ægypt for your preservation.
45.6: For it is two years since the famine began to be upon the earth, and yet five years remain, wherein there can be neither earing nor reaping.
45.7: And God sent me before, that you may be preserved upon the earth, and may have victuals to live.
45.8: Not by your counsel, but by the will of God was I sent hither: who hath made me as it were a father to Pharao, and lord of his whole house, and Prince in all the land of Ægypt.
45.9: Make haste and get ye up to my father, and you shall say to him: Thy son Joseph willeth thus: God hath made me lord of the whole land of Ægypt: come down to me, tarry not.
45.10: And thou shalt dwell in the land of Gessen: and thou shalt be near me thou and thy sons, and thy son's children, thy sheep, and thy herds, and all things that thou dost possess.
45.11: And there I will feed thee (for yet there are five years of famine remaining) lest both thou perish and thy house, and all things that thou dost possess.
45.12: Behold, your eyes, and the eyes of my brother Benjamin do see, that it is my mouth that speaketh unto you.
45.13: Report to my father my whole glory & all things that you have seen in Ægypt: make haste, and bring him to me.
45.14: And falling upon the neck of his brother Benjamin, embracing him he wept: he also in like manner weeping upon his neck.
45.15: And Joseph kissed all his brethren, and wept upon every one: after which things they were bold to speak unto him.
45.16: And it was heard of, and very famously reported abroad in the king's court: The brethren of Joseph are come: and Pharao was glad, and all his family.
45.17: And he spake to Joseph that he should command his brethren, saying: Loading your beasts go into the Land of Chanaan,
45.18: and take thence your father and kin, and come to me: and I will give you all the good things of Ægypt, that you may eat the marrow of the land.
45.19: Give commandment also that they take wains out of the land of Ægypt, for the carriage of their little ones and wives: and say: Take up your father, and make haste to come with all speed.
45.20: Neither do you leave any thing of your household stuff: for all the riches of Ægypt shall be yours.
45.21: And the sons of Israel did as it was commanded them. To whom Joseph gave wains according to Pharao's commandment, and victuals for the way.
45.22: He bade also to be brought up for every one two robes: but to Benjamin he gave three hundred pieces of silver with five robes of the best:
45.23: sending to his father as much money & raiment, adding besides them he asses that should carry of all the riches of Ægypt, and as many she asses, carrying wheat for the journey and bread.
45.24: Therefore he dismissed his brethren, and when they were departing he said to them: Be not angry in the way.
45.25: Who going up out of Ægypt, came into the land of Chanaan to their father Jacob.
45.26: And they told him saying: Joseph thy son is living: and he ruleth in all the Land of Ægypt. Which when Jacob heard, awaking as it were out of a heavy sleep, notwithstanding did not believe them.
45.27: They on the contrary side reported the whole order of the thing. And when he saw the wains and all things that he had sent, his spirit revived,
45.28: and he said: It sufficeth me if Joseph my son be living yet: I will go, and see him before I die.

46.1: And Israel taking his journey with all things that he had came to the well of the oath, and killing there victims to the God of his father Isaac,
46.2: he heard him by a vision of the night calling him, and saying unto him: Jacob, Jacob. To whom he answered: Lo here I am.
46.3: God said to him: I am the most mighty God of thy father: fear not, go down into Ægypt, for into a great nation will I make thee there.
46.4: I will go down with thee thither, and thence will I bring thee returning: Joseph also shall put his hands upon thine eyes.
46.5: And Jacob rose up from the well of the oath: and his sons took him up, with their little ones and wives in the wains, which Pharao had sent to carry the old man,
46.6: and all that he had possessed in the Land of Chanaan: and he came into Ægypt with all his seed,
46.7: His sons, and nephews, daughters, and all his progeny together.
46.8: And these are the names of the children of Israel, that entered into Ægypt, himself with his children. His first-begotten Ruben.
46.9: The sons of Ruben: Henoch and Phallu and Hesron and Charmi.
46.10: The sons of Simeon: Iamuel and Jamin and Ahod, and Jachin and Sohar, and Saul the Son of Chananitess.
46.11: The sons of Levi: Gerson and Caath and Merari.
46.12: The sons of Juda: Her & Onan and Sela and Phares and Zara. And Her and Onan died in the land of Chanaan. And there were sons born to Phares: Hesron and Hamul.
46.13: The sons of Issachar: Thola and Phua and Job and Semron.
46.14: The sons of Zabulon: Sared and Elon and Iahelel.
46.15: These are the sons of Lia, which she bare in Mesopotamia of Syria with Dina his daughter. All the souls of his sons and daughters are thirty three.
46.16: The sons of Gad: Sephion and Haggi and Siuni & Esebon and Heri and Arodi and Areli.
46.17: The sons of Aser: Jamne and Jesua and Jessui and Beria, Sara also their sister. The sons of Beria: Heber and Melchiel,
46.18: these be the sons of Zelpha, whom Laban gave to Lia his daughter, and these she bare to Jacob sixteen souls.
46.19: The sons of Rachel Jacob's wife: Joseph and Benjamin.
46.20: And there were sons born to Joseph in the Land of Ægypt, which Aseneth the daughter of Putiphar Priest of Heliopolis bare to him: Manasses and Ephraim.
46.21: The sons of Benjamin: Bela and Bechor and Asbel and Gera and Naaman and Echi and Ros and Mophim and Ophim and Ared.
46.22: These be the sons of Rachel, which she bare to Jacob: all the souls, fourteen.
46.24: The sons of Nephthali: Jaziel and Guni and Ieser and Sallem.
46.25: These be the sons of Bala, whom Laban gave to Rachel his daughter: and these she bare to Jacob; all the souls, seven.
46.26: All the souls, that entered with Jacob into Ægypt, and that came out of his thigh, besides his son's wives, sixty six.
46.27: And the sons of Joseph, that were born to him in the land of Ægypt, two souls, All the souls of the house of Jacob, that entered into Ægypt, were seventy.
46.28: And he sent Judas before him to Joseph, that he should tell him, and he should come into Gessen to meet him.
46.29: Wither when he was come, Joseph addressing his chariot went up to meet his father, unto the same place: and seeing him fell upon his neck, and as they embraced he wept.
46.30: And his father said to Joseph: Now will I die with joy, because I have seen thy face, and do leave thee alive.
46.31: But he spake to his brethren, and to all his father's house: I will go up, & will tell Pharao, and will say to him: My brethren, and my father's house that were in the Land of Chanaan, are come to me:
46.32: and the men are pastors of sheep, & their trade is to feed flocks: their cattle, & herds, and all that they could have, they have brought with them.
46.33: And when he shall call you & shall say: What is your trade?
46.34: You shall answer: We thy servants are pastors, from our infancy until this present, both we and our fathers. And this you shall say, that you may dwell in the Land of Gessen, because the Ægyptians detest all pastors of sheep.

47.1: Joseph therefore going in told Pharao, saying: My father and brethren, their sheep and herds, and all things that they possess, are come out of the Land of Chanaan; and behold they stay in the Land of Gessen.
47.2: The utmost also of his brethren five persons he presented before the king:
47.3: whom he asked: What trade have you? They answered: We thy servants are pastors of sheep, both we, and our fathers.
47.4: We are come to sojourn in the land, because there is no grass for thy servant's flocks, the famine being very sore in the land of Chanaan: and we desire thee to command that we thy servants may be in the Land of Gessen.
47.5: And the king therefore said to Joseph: Thy father and thy brethren are come to thee.
47.6: The Land of Ægypt is in thy sight: make them to dwell in the best place, & deliver them the Land of Gessen. And if so be thou know that there are industrious men among them, appoint them masters of my cattle.
47.7: After this Joseph brought in his father to the king, and set him before him: who blessed him,
47.8: and being asked of him: How many be the days of the years of thy life?
47.9: He answered: The days of the pilgrimage of my life are an hundred thirty years, few and evil, and they are not come to the days of my fathers, in which they were pilgrims.
47.10: And blessing the king, he went forth.
47.11: But Joseph gave possession to his father and his brethren in Ægypt, in the best place of the land, in Rhamesses, as Pharao had commanded.
47.12: And he nourished them, and all his father's house, allowing victuals to every one.
47.13: For in the whole world there wanted bread. & famine oppressed the land, especially of Ægypt and Chanaan.
47.14: Out of which he gathered together all the money for the selling of corn, & brought it in unto the king's treasure.
47.15: And when the buyers wanted money, all Ægypt came to Joseph, saying: Give us bread: why die we before thee, our money failing?
47.16: To whom he answered: Bring your cattle, and for them I will give you victuals, if you have not to pay.
47.17: Which when they had brought, he gave them sustenance for horses, and sheep, and oxen, and asses: and he sustained them that year for the exchange of the cattle.
47.18: And they came the second year, and said to him: We will not conceal from our lord, that our money failing, our cattle withal have failed: neither art thou ignorant, that we have nothing besides our bodies and land.
47.19: Why therefore shall we die in thy sight? Both we and our land will be thine: buy us to be the king's bondmen, & give us seed, lest for default of tillers the land be turned into a wilderness.
47.20: Joseph therefore bought all the Land of Ægypt, every man selling his possessions for the greatness of the famine. And he brought it under Pharao's hands,
47.21: and all the people thereof from the farthest ends of Ægypt, even to the uttermost coasts thereof,
47.22: saving the land of the Priests, which the king had delivered them: to whom also a certain allowance of victuals was given out of the common barns, and therefore they were not driven to sell their possessions.
47.23: Joseph therefore said to the people: Behold as you see, Pharao possesseth both you and your land: take seed, and sow the fields,
47.24: that you may have corn. The fifth part you shall give to the king: the other four Iam content you shall have for seed, & for food to your families & your children.
47.25: Who answered: Our life is in thy hand: only let our lord have a respect unto us, and we will gladly serve the king.
47.26: From that time until this present day in the whole land of Ægypt, the fifth part is payed to the kings, and it became as it were a law, saving the land of the Priests, which was free from this condition.
47.27: Israel therefore dwelt in Ægypt, that is, in the Land of Gessen, and possessed it: and was increased, & multiplied exceedingly.
47.28: And he lived in it seventeen years: and all the days of his life came to an hundred forty seven years.
47.29: And when he saw that the day of his death approached, he called his son Joseph, and said to him: If I have found grace in thy sight, put thy hand under my thigh: and thou shalt do me this mercy and truth. Not to bury me in Ægypt:
47.30: but I will sleep with my fathers, and take me away out of this land, & bury me in the sepulchre of my ancestors. To whom Joseph answered: I will do that thou hast commanded.
47.31: And he said: Swear then to me. Who swearing, Israel adored God, turning to the bed's head.

48.1: These things being so done, it was told Joseph that his father was sick: who, taking his two sons Manasses and Ephraim, went forward.
48.2: And it was told the old man: Behold thy son Joseph cometh to thee. Who being strengthened sat on his bed.
48.3: And Joseph being entered in to him, he said: God Almighty appeared to me in Luza, which is in the Land of Chanaan: and he blessed me,
48.4: and said: I will increase, and multiply thee, and make thee into multitudes of peoples: and I will give thee this land, and to thy seed after thee for an everlasting possession.
48.5: Thy two sons therefore which were born to thee in the Land of Ægypt before I came hither to thee, shall be mine: Ephraim and Manasses, as Ruben and Simeon shall be reputed to me.
48.6: But the rest begotten of thee after them, shall be thine, and shall be called by the name of their brethren in their possessions.
48.7: For unto me, when I came out of Mesopotamia, Rachel died in the land of Chanaan in the very journey, and it was spring time: and I entered into Ephrata, and buried her by the way side to Ephrata, which by another name is called Bethleem.
48.8: And seeing his sons he said to him: Who are these?
48.9: He answered: They are my sons, whom God hath given me in this place. Bring them, quoth he, to me, that I may bless them.
48.10: For Israel's eyes were dim by reason of very great age, and he could not see clearly. And when they were set beside him, kissing and embracing them,
48.11: he said to his son: I am not defrauded of thy sight, moreover God hath shewed me thy seed.
48.12: And when Joseph had taken them from his father's lap, he adored prostrate unto the ground.
48.13: And he set Ephraim on his right hand, that is, on the left hand of Israel: but Manasses on his own left hand, to wit, on his father's right hand, and put them near to him.
48.14: Who stretching forth his right hand, put it upon the head of Ephraim the younger brother: and the left upon the head of Manasses, that was the elder, changing hands.
48.15: And Jacob blessed the sons of Joseph, and said: God, in whose sight my fathers Abraham & Isaac have walked, God that feedeth me from my youth until this present day:
48.16: The Angel that delivereth me from all evils, bless these children: and be my name called upon them, the names also of my fathers Abraham, and Isaac, and grow they into a multitude upon the earth.
48.17: And Joseph seeing that his father had put his right hand upon the head of Ephraim, took it heavily: and taking his father's hand he went about to lift it from Ephraim's head & to remove it upon the head of Manasses.
48.18: And he said to his father: It is not convenient father so to be: because this is the first-begotten put thy right hand upon his head.
48.19: Who refusing, said: I know my son, I know: and this same indeed shall be into peoples, and shall be multiplied: but his younger brother shall be greater than he: and his seed shall grow into nations.
48.20: And he blessed them at that time, saying: In thee shall Israel be blessed, and it shall be said: God do unto thee as unto Ephraim, & as unto Manasses. And he set Ephraim before Manasses.
48.21: And he said to Joseph his son: Behold I die, & God will be with you, and will bring you back into the land of your fathers.
48.22: I do give thee one portion above thy brethren, which I took out of the hand of the Amorrhean with my sword and bow.

49.1: And Jacob called his sons, and said to them: Come together, that I may shew you the things that shall come to you in the last days.
49.2: Come together, and hear you sons of Jacob, hear ye Israel your father.
49.3: Ruben my first-begotten, thou art my strength, & the beginning of my sorrow: former in gifts, greater in Empire.
49.4: Thou art poured out as water, increase thou not: because thou didst ascend thy father's bed, and didst defile his couch.
49.5: Simeon and Levi brethren: vessels of iniquity warring.
49.6: Into their counsel come not my soul, and in their congregation be not my glory: because in their fury they slew a man, and in their willfulness they undermined a wall.
49.7: Cursed be their fury, because it is stubborn; and their indignation, because it is hard: I will divide them in Jacob, and will disperse them in Israel.
49.8: Judas, thee thy brethren shall praise: thy hand shall be in the neck of thine enemies: thy father's children shall adore thee.
49.9: Alion's whelp Judas: to the prey my son thou didst ascend: taking thy rest thou didst lie as a lion, and as it were a lioness, who shall raise him up?
49.10: The Sceptre Shall Not be Taken Away From Judas, And A duke Out of His Thigh, Till he do Come That is to be Sent, And The Same Shall be The Expectation of The Gentiles.
49.11: Tying to the vineyard his colt, and to the vine, o my son, his she ass. He shall wash his stole in wine, and in the blood of the grape his cloak.
49.12: His eyes are more beautiful than wine, and his teeth whiter than milk.
49.13: Zabulon shall dwell in the shore of the sea, and in the road of ships reaching as far as Sidon.
49.14: Issachar a strong ass lying at rest between the borders.
49.15: He saw rest that it was good; and the earth that it was very good: and he put under his shoulder to carry, and became serving under tributes.
49.16: Dan shall judge his own people, as also another tribe in Israel.
49.17: Be Dan a snake in the way, a serpent in the path, biting the hoofs of the horse, that his rider may fall backward.
49.18: I will expect Thy Salvation o Lord.
49.19: Gad, the girded shall fight before him: and himself shall be girded backward.
49.20: Aser, his bread is fat, and he shall give dainties to kings.
49.21: Nephthali, a hart let forth, and giving speeches of beauty.
49.22: Joseph a child increasing, increasing and comely to behold: the daughters coursed to and fro upon the wall.
49.23: But the dart men did exasperate him, and brawled, and envied him.
49.24: His bow sat upon the strong, and the bands of his arms and his hands were dissolved, by the hands of the Mighty of Jacob: thence came forth a pastor, the son of Israel.
49.25: The God of thy father shall be thy helper, and the Almighty shall bless thee with the blessings of heaven from above, with the blessings of the depth that lieth beneath, with the blessings of the paps and of the womb.
49.26: The blessings of thy father were strengthened with the blessings of his fathers: until the desire of the eternal hills came: be they upon the head of Joseph, and upon the crown of the Nazarite among his brethren.
49.27: Benjamin a ravening wolf, in the morning shall eat the prey, and in the evening shall divide the spoil.
49.28: All these in the tribes of Israel twelve: these things spake their father to them, and he blessed every one, with their proper blessings.
49.29: And he commanded them, saying: I am gathered unto my people: bury you me with my fathers in the double cave, which is in the field of Ephron the Hethite,
49.30: Against Mambre in the Land of Chanaan, which Abraham bought with the field of Ephron the Hethite for a possession to bury in.
49.31: There they buried him, and Sara his wife: there was Isaac buried with Rebecca his wife: there also Lia doth lie buried.
49.32: And when he had finished the precepts wherewith he instructed his sons, he plucked up his feet upon the bed, and died; and he was put unto his people.

50.1: Which Joseph seeing fell upon his father's face weeping and kissing him.
50.2: And he commanded his servants the physicians, that they should embalm his father with spices.
50.3: Who fulfilling his commandments, there passed forty days; for this was the manner of corpses embalmed, and Ægypt mourned him seventy days.
50.4: And the mourning time being expired, Joseph spake to the family of Pharao: If I have found grace in your sight, speak in the ears of Pharao:
50.5: for so much as my father did adjure me, saying: Behold I die, in my sepulchre which I digged for myself in the land of Chanaan, thou shalt bury me. I will go up therefore, and bury my father, and return.
50.6: And Pharao said to him: Go up and bury thy father as thou wast adjured.
50.7: Who going up, there went with him all the ancients of Pharao's house, and all the elders of the Land of Ægypt:
50.8: the house of Joseph with his brethren, saving their little ones, and the flocks and herds, which they had left in the Land of Gessen.
50.9: He had also in his train chariots and horsemen: and it became no small multitude.
50.10: And they came to the floor of Atad, which is situate beyond Jordan: where celebrating the exequies with great and vehement mourning, they spent full seven days.
50.11: Which when the Inhabiters of the Land of Chanaan had seen, they said: This is a great mourning unto the Ægyptians. And therefore the name of that place was called, The mourning of Ægypt.
50.12: Therefore the sons of Jacob did as he commanded them:
50.13: and carrying him into the Land of Chanaan, they buried him in the double cave, which Abraham had bought with the field for a possession to bury in of Ephron the Hethite against Mambre.
50.14: And Joseph returned into Ægypt with his brethren, and with all the train, his father being buried.
50.15: After whose death his brethren fearing, & talking one with another: Lest perhaps he be mindful of the injury which he suffered, and requite us all the evil that we have done;
50.16: they advertised him saying: Thy father commanded us before he died,
50.17: that we should say thus much to thee in his words: I beseech that thou forget the wicked fact of thy brethren, and the sin and malice which they have exercised against thee: we also desire thee, that to the servants of the God of thy father thou remit this iniquity. Whom when Joseph had heard he wept.
50.18: And his brethren came to him; and adoring prostrate on the ground they said: We are thy servants.
50.19: To whom he answered: Fear not; can we resist the will of God?
50.20: You thought evil against me, but God turned that into good, that he might exalt me, as presently you see, and might save many peoples.
50.21: Fear not: I will feed you and your little ones, and he comforted them, and spake gently & mildly.
50.22: And he dwelt in Ægypt, with all his father's house: and lived an hundred and ten years. And he saw the children of Ephraim unto the third generation. Also the children of Machir the son of Manasses were born in Joseph's knee.
50.23: Which things being done he spake to his brethren: After my death God will visit you, and will make you go up out of this land, to the land which he sware to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
50.24: And when he had adjured them and said. God will visit you carry my bones with you out of this place:
50.25: he died, being an hundred and ten years old. And being embalmed with spices, was put in a coffin in Ægypt.
